title: heliotropium bacciferum subsp. lignosum
intro: Heliotropium bacciferum subsp. lignosum, also known as 'Tree Heliotrope', is a plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Heliotropium bacciferum subsp. lignosum include its potential to treat skin conditions such as acne and wounds, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
